7808.history: Never commissioned into the Royal Netherlands Navy. Scuttled on 14 May 1940 in the Nieuwe Waterweg at Rotterdam to prevent her capture by the Germans. Raised by the Germans but repair and completion was found impossible. Scrapped at Hendrik-Ido-Ambacht.

7809.built_by: Koninklijke Maatschappij De Schelde (Vlissingen (Flushing), The Netherlands)
7809.history: Blown up on the slip on 17 May 1940 to prevent her capture by the Germans after several attemps to launch and tow her to England her had failed.

7810.history: Loaned to the Polish Navy before completion as Krakowiak. Returned in September 1946. Scrapped at Grays in March 1959.

7811.history: Loaned to the Polish Navy before completion on 30 May 1941 and commissioned as Kujawiak on 17 June 1941. Kujawiak was mined and sunk off Malta on 16 June 1942.

7812.built_by: John Brown Shipbuilding & Engineering Company Ltd. (Clydebank, Scotland)
7812.history: HMS Avon Vale was allocated to the Royal Hellenic Navy as Aegion from March 1944 until May 1944. She was never commissioned into the R.H.N. due to the mutinous state of the Greek Navy.

7813.history: HMS Cowdray was allocated to the Royal Hellenic Navy as Admiral Hastings from March 1944 until June 1944. She was never commissioned into the R.H.N. due to the mutinous state of the Greek Navy.

7814.built_by: Swan Hunter and Wigham Richardson Ltd. (Wallsend-on-Tyne, U.K.): Wallsend
7814.history: Transferred to the Royal Hellenic Navy before completion and renamed Adrias. Adrias was mined off Kalymnos on 22 October 1943. The ship lost her entire bow and was declared a constructive total loss. Scrapped in 1945.

7815.history: Transferred to the Royal Hellenic Navy before completion and renamed Kanaris. Returned to the Royal Navy in 1959. Scrapped in 1960.

7816.built_by: Swan Hunter and Wigham Richardson Ltd. (Wallsend-on-Tyne, U.K.): Wallsend
7816.history: Transferred to the Royal Hellenic Navy before completion and renamed Miaoulis. Returned to the Royal Navy in 1959. Scrapped in 1960.

7817.built_by: Swan Hunter and Wigham Richardson Ltd. (Wallsend-on-Tyne, U.K.): Wallsend
7817.history: Transferred to the Royal Hellenic Navy before completion and renamed Pindos. Returned to the Royal Navy in 1959. Scrapped in 1960.

7818.built_by: Caledon Shipbuilding & Engineering Co. Ltd. (Dundee, Scotland) : B.T.H.
commanders: Lt. George Sutherland Sinclair, DSO, DSC, RNR (May 1945 - 29 May 1946)

7820.built_by: Swan Hunter and Wigham Richardson Ltd. (Wallsend-on-Tyne, U.K.)
7820.history: Construction as Loch class frigate cancelled. Completed as depot ship Derby Haven on 2 August 1945.

7821.built_by: Swan Hunter and Wigham Richardson Ltd. (Wallsend-on-Tyne, U.K.)
7821.history: Construction as Loch class frigate cancelled. Completed as depot ship Woodbridge Haven on 19 October 1945.

7828.built_by: Swan Hunter and Wigham Richardson Ltd. (Wallsend-on-Tyne, U.K.)

7829.built_by: Swan Hunter and Wigham Richardson Ltd. (Wallsend-on-Tyne, U.K.)

7830.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)

7831.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)

7832.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)

7833.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)

7834.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)

7835.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)

7836.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)

7838.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)

7839.built_by: Swan Hunter and Wigham Richardson Ltd. (Wallsend-on-Tyne, U.K.)

7849.built_by: Blyth Shipbuilding & Drydock Co. Ltd. (Blyth, U.K.)

7850.built_by: Blyth Shipbuilding & Drydock Co. Ltd. (Blyth, U.K.)

7851.built_by: Blyth Shipbuilding & Drydock Co. Ltd. (Blyth, U.K.)

7852.built_by: Blyth Shipbuilding & Drydock Co. Ltd. (Blyth, U.K.)

7874.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)
7874.history: Cancelled in 1945 and scrapped on the slip.

7875.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)
7875.history: Construction as Bay class frigate was cancelled. Completed as dispatch vessel HMS Surprise on 9 September 1946. Scrapped in June 1965.

7876.built_by: Blyth Shipbuilding & Drydock Co. Ltd. (Blyth, U.K.)
7876.history: Construction as Bay class frigate was cancelled. Completed as dispatch vessel HMS Alert on 24 October 1946. Sold to be broken up for scrap in October 1971. She arrived at Inverkeithing to be scrapped on 31 October 1971.

7877.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)
7877.history: Construction as Bay class frigate was cancelled. Completed by Chatham Dockyard as survey vessel HMS Dampier on 6 June 1948. Scrapped in 1969.

7878.history: Construction as Bay class frigate was cancelled. Completed as survey vessel HMS Dalrymple on 10 September 1949. Transferred to Portugal in 1966 and renamed Afonso de Albuquerque.

7879.history: Construction as Bay class frigate was cancelled. Completed as survey vessel HMS Cook on 20 July 1950. Sold to be broken up for scrap in May 1968.

7880.history: Construction as Bay class frigate was cancelled. Completed as survey vessel HMS Owen on 23 September 1949. Sold to be broken up for scrap in July 1970.

7881.history: Transferred to the South African Navy on 10 June 1945. Returned to the Royal Navy in January 1946. Scrapped on 29 March 1955.

7882.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)
7882.history: Transferred to the South African Navy on 26 July 1945. Returned to the Royal Navy in January 1946. Scrapped on 26 February 1955.

7883.history: Transferred to the Royal Netherlands Navy during construction. Decommissioned by them in 1960.

7884.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)
7884.history: HMS Strule was transferred to the Free French Navy on 25 September 1944 and was renamed Croix de Lorraine. Decommissioned in 1961.

7885.built_by: Smiths Dock Co., Ltd. (South Bank-on-Tees, U.K.)
7885.history: Transferred to the F.N.F.L. (Free French) on 15 October 1944 and renamed Tonkinois. Decommissioned in 1961.

7886.history: Transferred to the F.N.F.L. (Free French) on 21 January 1944 and renamed L'Aventure. Decommissioned in 1964.

7887.built_by: Blyth Shipbuilding & Drydock Co. Ltd. (Blyth, U.K.)
7887.history: Transferred to the F.N.F.L. (Free French) on 3 March 1944 and renamed L'Escarmouche. Decommissioned in 1960.

7888.history: Transferred to the United States Navy before completion. Decommissioned by the United States Navy on 14 January 1946. Sold on 13 June 1946 to Argentina and renamed Hercules.

7889.history: Transferred to the F.N.F.L. (Free French) in February 1944 and renamed La Découverte. Decommissioned in 1959.

7890.built_by: Blyth Shipbuilding & Drydock Co. Ltd. (Blyth, U.K.)
7890.history: Transferred to the F.N.F.L. (Free French) in 1944 and renamed La Surprise. Transferred to Morocco in 1964 to serve as Royal Yacht and renamed Al Maouna.

7891.history: Laid down as HMS Annan (i). Allocated to the Royal Canadian Navy during construction. Transferred to the United States Navy during construction on 20 July 1942. Commissioned as USS Natchez on 16 December 1942. Decommissioned on 11 October 1945. Stricken at an unknown date. Sold on 29 July 1947. Resold on 19 March 1948 to the Dominican Republic being renamed Juan Pablo Doarte (F-102). Juan Pablo Doarte was sunk in 1957.

7892.history: Laid down as HMS Annan (i). Allocated to the Royal Canadian Navy during construction. Transferred to the United States Navy during construction on 20 July 1942. Commissioned as USS Natchez on 16 December 1942. Decommissioned on 11 October 1945. Stricken at an unknown date. Sold on 29 July 1947. Resold on 19 March 1948 to the Dominican Republic being renamed Juan Pablo Doarte (F-102). Juan Pablo Doarte was sunk in 1957.

commanders: T/A/Lt.Cdr. Thomas William Fearne Bolland, RNR (1 Dec 1943 - 15 Jan 1946)

7894.history: Returned to the Royal Navy on 30 April 1947. Sold in July 1947.

7895.history: Transferred to the Royal Canadian Navy before completion. Decommissioned and returned to the Royal Navy on 17 June 1945. Sold on 17 May 1947. Became the Dutch merchantile Albert W. Vinke in 1950. Served until 1964. Broken up at Cape Town, South Africa in 1965

7896.built_by: Davie Shipbuilding and Repairing Co. Ltd. (Lauzon, Quebec, Canada)
7896.history: Transferred to the Royal Canadian Navy before completion. Lost in Canadian service when rammed and sunk by the Dutch freighter Zypenberg in dense fog off the Grand Banks.

7897.built_by: Davie Shipbuilding and Repairing Co. Ltd. (Lauzon, Quebec, Canada)
7897.history: Transferred to the Royal Canadian Navy before completion. Decommissioned and returned to the Royal Navy on 27 June 1945. Scrapped at Llanelly on 1 January 1948.

7898.built_by: Davie Shipbuilding and Repairing Co. Ltd. (Lauzon, Quebec, Canada)
7898.history: Transferred to the Royal Canadian Navy before completion. Decommissioned and returned to the Royal Navy on 8 June 1945. Scrapped at Middlesborough, U.K. in August 1947.

7899.history: Transferred to the Royal Canadian Navy before completion. Decommissioned and returned to the Royal Navy on 25 June 1945. Became the merchantile Olympic Runner in 1950, Otori Maru 10 in 1956 and Kyo Maru No. 16 in 1959. Last in Lloyd's Register for 1972 / 1973.

7900.history: Transferred to the Royal Canadian Navy before completion. Decommissioned on and returned to the Royal Navy on 22 June 1945. Scrapped in November 1950.

7901.history: Transferred to the Royal Canadian Navy before completion. Decommissioned and returned to the Royal Navy on 31 May 1945. Scrapped at Inverkeithing on 20 September 1949.

7902.history: Transferred to the Royal Canadian Navy before completion. Decommissioned and returned to the Royal Navy on 12 June 1945. Sold in 1948 to Kosmos and became the merchantile Milliam Kihl. Rebuildt as bouy-boat in October 1948. Refitted as whaler in 1951 in Kiel, Germany. Laid up in 1960/1961. Last drifting season 1964/1965. Laid up again in Sandefjord. Sold to Norwegian shipbreakers in Grimstad in 1966.

7903.built_by: Davie Shipbuilding and Repairing Co. Ltd. (Lauzon, Quebec, Canada)
7903.history: Transferred to the Royal Canadian Navy before completion. HMCS Spikenard (Lt.Cdr. H.G. Shadforth, R.C.N.R.) was torpedoed and sunk by U-136, south of Iceland, while escorting convoy SC-67. There were only eight survivors.

7904.history: Transferred on loan to the the Royal New Zealand Navy upon completion. Returned to the Royal Navy in 1948. Scrapped at Grays in August 1951.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. John Henry Seelye, RNZNVR (20 Dec 1943 - 16 Dec 1944)
Lt.Cdr. Peter Phipps, DSC, RNZNVR (16 Dec 1944 - )

7905.history: Transferred on loan to the the Royal New Zealand Navy upon completion. Returned to the Royal Navy in 1948. Scrapped at Dunston in June 1951.
commanders: Lt.Cdr. John Alexander Rhind, RNZNVR (15 Feb 1944 - 9 Feb 1945)
T/Lt. Nigel Deerness Blair, RNZNVR (9 Feb 1945 - )

7906.history: Loaned to the Royal Indian Navy as HMIS Sind on 24 August 1945. Returned to the Royal Navy on 17 May 1946. Sold to the Thai Navy in 1947 being renamed Prasae. Prasae was beached during a snowstorm on the Korean east coast on 7 January 1951. After attempts to pull her off the beach had failed she was scuttled on the 13th.
commanders: T/A/Lt.Cdr. Leonard George Prowse, RINVR (24 Mar 1945 - Oct 1945 ?)

7907.history: Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 5 January 1946.

7909.history: Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 20 March 1946.

7910.history: Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 5 January 1946.

7911.history: Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 21 June 1946.

7912.history: Transferred to the Royal Navy under lend-lease. Returned to the United States Navy on 11 June 1946.

7913.built_by: Swan Hunter and Wigham Richardson Ltd. (Wallsend-on-Tyne, U.K.)
7913.history: Never served in the Royal Navy. Transferred to the Royal Norwegian Navy upon completion.

7914.history: Construction to corvette cancelled. Completed as convoy rescue ship Empire Lifeguard. Scrapped on 22 July 1955.

7915.history: Construction as corvette cancelled. Completed as convoy rescue ship Empire Peacemaker. Scrapped in June 1955.

7916.history: Construction as corvette cancelled. Completed as convoy rescue ship Empire Comfort. Scrapped in June 1955.

7917.history: Construction as corvette cancelled. Completed as convoy rescue ship Empire Rest. Scrapped in June 1952.

7918.history: Construction as corvette cancelled. Completed as convoy rescue ship Empire Shelter. Scrapped in July 1955.

7934.history: Never commissioned into the Royal Navy.

7935.history: Never commissioned into the Royal Navy.
